steps.title: Select two points from the trend line
steps.content:
From the scatterplot, read two clear points on the upward trend:
  • (20% concentration, 1150 kg/m³)
  • (80% concentration, 1350 kg/m³)

steps.title: Calculate the slope of the trend line
steps.content:
Use the slope formula $$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$$ with the selected points.
$$m = \frac{1350 - 1150}{80 - 20} = \frac{200}{60} = \frac{10}{3}$$

steps.title: Find the y‑intercept of the line
steps.content:
Substitute one point and the slope into $$y = mx + b$$ to solve for $$b$$.
Using (20, 1150):
$$1150 = \frac{10}{3} \cdot 20 + b$$
$$b = 1150 - \frac{200}{3} = \frac{3450 - 200}{3} = \frac{3250}{3}$$

steps.title: Predict density at 60% concentration and match to choices
steps.content:
Insert $$x = 60$$ into the line equation.
$$y = \frac{10}{3} \cdot 60 + \frac{3250}{3} = 200 + \frac{3250}{3} = \frac{600 + 3250}{3} = \frac{3850}{3} \approx 1283.3$$
The value 1283.3 kg/m³ is closest to 1,300 kg/m³ among the options.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
